entry.title: Strengthening the SAP Talent Pipeline: How SAP Partners and Academia Can Connect
entry.content:
In the rapidly changing world of technology and digital transformation, maintaining a skilled workforce is crucial. SAP partners rely on talented, well-prepared consultants to implement and optimize SAP solutions for their clients. SAP University Alliances (UA), a global academic initiative of SAP, plays a key role in nurturing this talent pipeline within the SAP ecosystem.

To raise awareness among SAP partners about the potential of SAP University Alliances, SAP offers a strategic way to shape their candidate pool. SAP partners can now collaborate with over 2,700 universities worldwide through the UA global network. This opportunity is explicitly listed in on one of the main tools for partners – the SAP Partner Benefits Catalog (link accessible only by authorised SAP partners), which serves as a guide for go-to-market (GTM) strategies and helps partners engage with and grow within the SAP ecosystem.

Benefits of Partnering with SAP University Alliances

By partnering with SAP University Alliances, service partners can tap into a global network of academic institutions, allowing them to recruit interns who are both enthusiastic and well-prepared for SAP consulting roles. This collaboration helps guide students from UA-member schools into SAP consulting careers, ensuring a steady pipeline of talent for SAP partners.

The benefits are mutual:

  • Interns gain real-world SAP experience, professional training and certifications, giving them a competitive edge in the job market.
  • SAP Partners onboard interns who are already equipped with foundational SAP skills, enhancing their ability to contribute to current and future projects.

Collaboration Opportunities with SAP University Alliances

The SAP Partner Benefits Catalog (PBC) outlines various ways partners can engage with SAP University Alliances member schools in the section “Connect with young talents through SAP University Alliances”, the listed in PBC options include:

  • Networking opportunities: Join designated signature events of SAP University Alliances to matchmake with academic community and relevant institutions in your region
  • Guest Lectures: Deliver guest lectures to directly interact with talented students.
  • Internships: Offer internships to high-achieving students and gain access to emerging talent.
  • Sponsoring Technical Teaching Infrastructure: Increase brand awareness by covering fees for SAP systems used in academic teaching or setting up learning environments for universities leveraging a system infrastructure of your choice.
  • Site Visits: Invite students and lecturers to visit your facilities to showcase your work environment and corporate culture.

To learn more about collaborating with academic institutions, please refer to the SAP Partner Benefits Catalog.

Highlighting the Student Internships Sponsor Plan

One standout initiative in the SAP Partner Benefits Catalog is the Student Internships Sponsor Plan (link accessible only by authorised SAP partners) which focuses on nurturing a talent pipeline and supporting students early in their careers. As part of this program, SAP offers reimbursement to partners for SAP Learning Hub, Partner Edition subscriptions, provided their interns complete at least one certification during their internship.

This initiative aligns with SAP’s goal of helping interns launch their careers in SAP consulting, while also positioning SAP partners as attractive employers. By offering comprehensive learning resources and certification opportunities, SAP empowers interns and junior consultants with skills that are directly applicable to SAP projects. At the same time, SAP partners benefit from being seen as forward-thinking companies committed to the development of the next generation of SAP professionals.

For more information on how your organization can participate in this program and apply for reimbursement, please refer to the SAP Partner Benefits Catalog.

Using the SAP Partner Finder for Effective Collaborations

While the SAP Partner Benefits Catalog offers a streamlined way for partners to connect with universities, there is an additional tool available to help faculty members and students find and engage with SAP partners: the SAP Partner Finder . This tool allows those interested to independently identify SAP partners within their region and industry sectors of relevance. By proactively reaching out to these partners, one can strengthen students' career prospects and deepen SAP-related learning at their institutions. Direct partnerships with local SAP partners provide students with valuable real-world insights and hands-on experiences, helping them build the connections and skills needed for future SAP consulting roles.

Building a Future Together

At SAP, we believe that fostering a culture of learning, collaboration, and growth within the SAP ecosystem is essential for ensuring a future led by highly trained professionals. By leveraging resources like the SAP Partner Benefits Catalog and the SAP Partner Finder, universities and SAP partners can work together to create meaningful opportunities for students. These partnerships not only prepare students for successful careers in SAP consulting but also provide SAP partners with a skilled, ready-to-work talent pool. Together, universities, students, and SAP partners are building a strong foundation for the future of the industry.

Learn more about SAP University Alliances: SAP University Alliances | SAP Next Gen Community.

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?

tables:
lt_ - local variable table
mt_ - class member variable table
gt_ - global variable table

simple type variables:
lv_ - local variable 
mv_ - class member variable
gv_ - global variable

Ability to distinguish local, class member and global variables is an important one, at least for me.
Although i try to use as little as possible global variables..

entry.title: Re: roles for user in SU01 for to send email
entry.content:
Hello,

You need to assign email send authorizations.

The authorization object S_OC_SEND and (as values) the permitted communication methods and the maximum number of recipients to which a user may address a message are assigned to you. The authorizations required for sending are, by default, contained in the role SAP_BC_SRV_USER for the end user of the Business Workplace.

https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/026f8a31bca948a086e995ce267f4e38/4a25a37c08a81c67e10000000a42189c.html

entry.title: Production Connector -- Dispatch Message Error - Exception during the start of the destination
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

at the moment we are facing a problem where we cannot make any progress with the given logs and error messages.

Any hint as to whether it is a certificate problem, a Cloud Connector problem or whether there are additional ways of isolating the error will help us.

Process setup

OPC-UA as a shopfloor system sends an indicator to the DM.
In the DM there is an Automatic Trigger Subscription with an assigned PPD, which waits for changes to the indicator.
When the indicator is changed in the OPC server, the subscription is already called and stored as a message in the correct agent instance of the Production Connector.
However, the agent instance then has a problem when calling the PPD towards the DM.

  • The connections in “Configure Production Connectivity” are already maintained and have the status running
  • We double checked the BTP Destination SAP_DMC_DEFAULT_SERVICE_KEY
  • We worked through Troubleshooting - Configure Production Connectivity App | SAP Help Portal
  • We have looked at the Production Connector logs (no further indications of a connection error, only information entries)

 

Our current state of knowledge

Certificate from the Production Connector in the DM compared to the automatically generated client proxy to the PPD

User Settings in Production Connector Config

Message in the agent instance from the subscription that cannot be sent to the PPD destination.



Best regards

entry.title: Industry Sector field in Product Master in S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I need to replicate Product master data from S4HANA Private Cloud to S4HANA Public Cloud. I found an API for this, but I'm having trouble with the "Industry" field. In the Private Cloud, this field is in the MARA table (T137), but it's not in the Manage Product Master Fiori app (F1602) in the Public Cloud. The API includes the field, but I can't find a way to manage it in the Public Cloud.

Is this intentional, or am I missing something? This is important because if the industry values differ or if the Public Cloud expects a blank value, the replication will fail.

entry.title: Synch local dimension members between models
entry.content:
Hi experts,

for each planning model I´ve a parallel backup model. Data actions are backing up data from the planning model to the backup model. This works as long as local dimension members are synched between both models. If not, data is not copied.

Now I´m searching for a generic solution how to replicate local dimension members from a planning model to the corresponding backup model.

My ideas:

a) Export job for dimension -> functionality not existing

b) Transport network -> not possible to import dimension data to a new local dimension

c) Create subscription on planning model -> Can only see option to read booked dimension members based on fact data, means it´s not a generic solution. Versions and measures might be added later to the model, but replication of local members should still work without a change. Or is there another solution with subscription / data export API / data import API?

d) API Scripting in a story: Haven´t checked this until now. However, if it´s possible how could it be executed scheduled within SAC?

Does anybody see a (generic) solution to replicate local dimension members between planning models?

BR Thomas

entry.title: Re: EPMDimensionOverrideRange
entry.content:
OK got it working even with table ranges but seems your range can't contain formulas.

To illustrate. Referencing the yellow cells which are a formula combing text and cell contents to add the BAS(*) causes excel to crash 

Yellow override - =EPMDimensionOverrideRange("000","PROFITCENTRE",Table1[Manual Dimension Override List for Range])

Yellow cell ref range = formula to add BAS(*) to the member entered in the first column "="BAS("&[@[Manual Dimension Override list]]&")""

 

Blue ticked override - "=EPMDimensionOverrideRange("000","PROFITCENTRE",Table1[Manual Dimension Override List for Range HardTyped])"

Blue ticked cell ref range = Hard typed "BAS(P1636400)" etc

Seems a shame as it means I'll have to instruct the end users to enter the member with BAS(*) wrapped around it. Would be far simpler to allow them to enter the member that they recognise and then for me to add the right text in another cell and then to ref that cell in the dimension override

entry.title: Re: How many times has the output been printed?
entry.content:
Hello,

A spool creates an entry in table TSP01 and when an output request(print) is created there is an entry in table TSP02. If the spool is printed more then once there will be additional entries created in table TSP02. As below spool number 193 was printed twice and these two entries are created in table TSP02.

TSP02:
PJIDENT PJNUMMER
193 1
193 2

But when spools are deleted, the entries in table TSP01 and TSP02 are deleted.

There is a Customer Enhancement for Spool Accounting available as per the SAP Help Portal URL below

https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_crm700_ehp01/7.0.1.15/en-US/e0/989de87a6111d39a1d0000e83dd9fc/frameset.htm 
Customer Enhancement for Spool Accounting

Perhaps you could implement this.

Regards,
Aidan

entry.title: Graphic colors get distored after trying to remove grey background and converting to BMP
entry.content:
Hello,

I have this image with a white background, and it has shadows. Here is a small part of it to give you an idea and still keep the clients privacy:

It is a png image (though I also have it in BMP). When I, after converting it to BMP in paint, upload it in SAP in SE78, it gets a grey background.
I have found several possible solutions for this:

I used GIMP to add an alpha layer, added a white background, then exported it as 24 BMP. It still gets the grey background then.
And I tried opening that in paint, saving it as 256 BMP, but then the colors get super distorted, like this:

 

 instead of the colors smoothly transitioning from one to the other. 

I tried removing the white in GIMP but that also didnt really work (mostly, probably, because GIMP is new to me and when I removed the white, the image became really vague and whitened. 

Does anyone know what I could do to fix this, or where to look?
Thank you so much in advance.

Kind regards,
Romy

entry.title: How to Consume a class as and API or Consume it as an cds view
entry.content:
Hi All,
I have created a class in Eclipse ADT (ABAP CLoud Project )
1.created a CDS View Consuming the cds view Created a Class where i added all Logics 
2. Created a Entity defining the Class 
3. Created Service Binding and Definition defining reference to the entity
3. On Fiori preview i am unable to see data but the fields i can  

Class Code 

CLASS zstockcard DEFINITION PUBLIC CREATE PUBLIC.

 

PUBLIC SECTION.

* INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun.

INTERFACES if_rap_query_provider.

 

DATA:lv_mandt TYPE sy-mandt,

out TYPE REF TO if_oo_adt_classrun_out.

 

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_material_data,

material TYPE zstockcardcdsview-material,

plant TYPE zstockcardcdsview-plant,

postingdate TYPE zstockcardcdsview-postingdate,

quantity_in_base TYPE zstockcardcdsview-quantityinbaseunit,

stockquantity TYPE zstockcardcdsview-matlwrhsstkqtyinmatlbaseunit,

unitprice TYPE zstockcardcdsview-unitprice,

currentdate TYPE zstockcardcdsview-currentdate,

END OF ty_material_data.

 

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_material_data1,

material TYPE zstockcardcdsview-material,

plant TYPE zstockcardcdsview-plant,

quantity_in_base TYPE zstockcardcdsview-quantityinbaseunit,

stockquantity TYPE string,

END OF ty_material_data1.

 

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_90_days,

material TYPE zstockcardcdsview-material,

plant TYPE zstockcardcdsview-plant,

unitprice TYPE zstockcardcdsview-unitprice,

quantity_in_base TYPE zstockcardcdsview-quantityinbaseunit,

END OF ty_90_days.

 

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_180_days,

material TYPE zstockcardcdsview-material,

plant TYPE zstockcardcdsview-plant,

unitprice TYPE zstockcardcdsview-unitprice,

quantity_in_base TYPE zstockcardcdsview-quantityinbaseunit,

END OF ty_180_days.

 

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_above180_days,

material TYPE zstockcardcdsview-material,

plant TYPE zstockcardcdsview-plant,

unitprice TYPE zstockcardcdsview-unitprice,

quantity_in_base TYPE zstockcardcdsview-quantityinbaseunit,

END OF ty_above180_days.

 

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_output_data,

stockquantity TYPE string,

unitprice TYPE string,

plant TYPE werks_d,

material TYPE matnr,

qty_0to90days TYPE string,

value_0to90days TYPE string,

qty_90to180days TYPE string,

value_90to180days TYPE string,

qty_above180days TYPE string,

value_above180days TYPE string,

END OF ty_output_data.

.

DATA: lt_90days TYPE TABLE OF ty_90_days,

lt_180days TYPE TABLE OF ty_180_days,

lt_above180 TYPE TABLE OF ty_above180_days,

lt_stddata TYPE TABLE OF ty_material_data,

ls_stddata TYPE ty_material_data,

lt_stkstd TYPE TABLE OF ty_material_data1,

ls_stkstd TYPE ty_material_data1,

lt_output TYPE TABLE OF ty_output_data,

ls_output TYPE ty_output_data,

lv_days_diff TYPE int4,

lv_qty_left TYPE zstockcardcdsview-matlwrhsstkqtyinmatlbaseunit,

lv_index TYPE string.

 

ENDCLASS.

 

CLASS zstockcard IMPLEMENTATION.

 

 

METHOD if_rap_query_provider~select.

"in Local testing Method if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

 

 

SELECT material,

plant,

postingdate,

quantityinbaseunit,

matlwrhsstkqtyinmatlbaseunit,

unitprice,

currentdate

FROM zstockcardcdsview

INTO TABLE @lt_stddata UP TO 10000 ROWS.

 

TRY.

CASE io_request->get_entity_id( ).

WHEN 'ZStockAgeEntity'.

IF io_request->is_data_requested( ).

DATA(lv_offset) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_offset( ).

DATA(lv_page_size) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_page_size( ).

DATA(lv_max_rows) = COND #(

WHEN lv_page_size = if_rap_query_paging=>page_size_unlimited THEN 0

ELSE lv_page_size

).

 

 

" Categorize data into lt_90days, lt_180days, and lt_above180

LOOP AT lt_stddata INTO ls_stddata.

 

lv_days_diff = ls_stddata-currentdate - ls_stddata-postingdate.

 

IF lv_days_diff BETWEEN 0 AND 90.

READ TABLE lt_90days INTO DATA(ls_90days) WITH KEY material = ls_stddata-material plant = ls_stddata-plant.

lv_index = sy-tabix.

IF sy-subrc = 0.

ls_90days-quantity_in_base = ls_90days-quantity_in_base + ls_stddata-quantity_in_base.

MODIFY lt_90days FROM ls_90days INDEX lv_index.

ELSE.

MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_stddata TO ls_90days.

INSERT ls_90days INTO TABLE lt_90days.

ENDIF.

 

ELSEIF lv_days_diff BETWEEN 91 AND 180.

READ TABLE lt_180days INTO DATA(ls_180days) WITH KEY material = ls_stddata-material plant = ls_stddata-plant.

lv_index = sy-tabix.

IF sy-subrc = 0.

ls_180days-quantity_in_base = ls_180days-quantity_in_base + ls_stddata-quantity_in_base.

MODIFY lt_180days FROM ls_180days INDEX lv_index.

ELSE.

MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_stddata TO ls_180days.

INSERT ls_180days INTO TABLE lt_180days.

ENDIF.

 

ELSE.

READ TABLE lt_above180 INTO DATA(ls_above180) WITH KEY material = ls_stddata-material plant = ls_stddata-plant.

lv_index = sy-tabix.

IF sy-subrc = 0.

ls_above180-quantity_in_base = ls_above180-quantity_in_base + ls_stddata-quantity_in_base.

MODIFY lt_above180 FROM ls_above180 INDEX lv_index.

ELSE.

MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_stddata TO ls_above180.

INSERT ls_above180 INTO TABLE lt_above180.

ENDIF.

 

ENDIF.

CLEAR:ls_stddata,ls_90days,ls_180days,ls_above180,lv_index.

ENDLOOP.

 

LOOP AT lt_stddata INTO ls_stddata.

MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_stddata TO ls_stkstd.

COLLECT ls_stkstd INTO lt_stkstd.

ENDLOOP.

* MOVE-CORRESPONDING lt_stddata to lt_stkstd.

 

LOOP AT lt_stkstd INTO ls_stkstd.

CLEAR: ls_output, lv_qty_left.

 

" Initialize remaining quantity to process

lv_qty_left = ls_stkstd-stockquantity.

 

" Process 0 to 90 days

LOOP AT lt_90days INTO ls_90days WHERE material = ls_stkstd-material AND plant = ls_stkstd-plant.

IF lv_qty_left <= ls_90days-quantity_in_base.

ls_output-material = ls_stkstd-material.

ls_output-plant = ls_stkstd-plant.

ls_output-stockquantity = ls_stkstd-stockquantity.

ls_output-qty_0to90days = lv_qty_left.

ls_output-unitprice = ls_90days-unitprice.

ls_output-value_0to90days = lv_qty_left * ls_90days-unitprice.

lv_qty_left = 0.

EXIT.

ELSE.

ls_output-material = ls_stkstd-material.

ls_output-plant = ls_stkstd-plant.

ls_output-stockquantity = ls_stkstd-stockquantity.

ls_output-qty_0to90days = ls_90days-quantity_in_base.

ls_output-unitprice = ls_90days-unitprice.

ls_output-value_0to90days = ls_output-qty_0to90days * ls_90days-unitprice.

lv_qty_left = lv_qty_left - ls_90days-quantity_in_base.

ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.

 

" Process 91 to 180 days

LOOP AT lt_180days INTO ls_180days WHERE material = ls_stkstd-material AND plant = ls_stkstd-plant.

IF lv_qty_left <= ls_180days-quantity_in_base.

ls_output-material = ls_stkstd-material.

ls_output-plant = ls_stkstd-plant.

ls_output-stockquantity = ls_stkstd-stockquantity.

ls_output-qty_90to180days = lv_qty_left.

ls_output-unitprice = ls_180days-unitprice.

ls_output-value_90to180days = lv_qty_left * ls_180days-unitprice.

lv_qty_left = 0.

EXIT.

ELSE.

ls_output-material = ls_stkstd-material.

ls_output-plant = ls_stkstd-plant.

ls_output-stockquantity = ls_stkstd-stockquantity.

ls_output-qty_90to180days = ls_180days-quantity_in_base.

ls_output-unitprice = ls_180days-unitprice.

ls_output-value_90to180days = ls_output-qty_90to180days * ls_180days-unitprice.

lv_qty_left = lv_qty_left - ls_180days-quantity_in_base.

ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.

 

" Process above 180 days

LOOP AT lt_above180 INTO ls_above180 WHERE material = ls_stkstd-material AND plant = ls_stkstd-plant.

IF lv_qty_left <= ls_above180-quantity_in_base.

ls_output-material = ls_stkstd-material.

ls_output-plant = ls_stkstd-plant.

ls_output-stockquantity = ls_stkstd-stockquantity.

ls_output-qty_above180days = lv_qty_left.

ls_output-unitprice = ls_above180-unitprice.

ls_output-value_above180days = lv_qty_left * ls_above180-unitprice.

lv_qty_left = 0.

EXIT.

ELSE.

ls_output-material = ls_stkstd-material.

ls_output-plant = ls_stkstd-plant.

ls_output-stockquantity = ls_stkstd-stockquantity.

ls_output-qty_above180days = ls_above180-quantity_in_base.

ls_output-unitprice = ls_above180-unitprice.

ls_output-value_above180days = ls_output-qty_above180days * ls_above180-unitprice.

lv_qty_left = lv_qty_left - ls_above180-quantity_in_base.

ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.

APPEND ls_output TO lt_output.

ENDLOOP.

** out->write( EXPORTING data = lt_output name = 'Table display' ).

IF io_request->is_total_numb_of_rec_requested( ).

io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( lt_output ) ).

ENDIF.

SORT lt_output ASCENDING BY material plant.

io_response->set_data( lt_output ).

 

ENDIF.

ENDCASE.

CATCH cx_rap_query_provider.

ENDTRY.

ENDMETHOD.

 

ENDCLASS.


Entity-

@EndUserText.label: 'Entity to define class for stock card report'

@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZSTOCKCARD'

define custom entity ZStockAgeEntity

{

 

key plant : abap.char(4);

key material : matnr;

stockquantity : abap.string;

unitprice : abap.string;

qty_0to90days : abap.string;

value_0to90days : abap.string;

qty_90to180days : abap.string;

value_90to180days : abap.string;

qty_above180days : abap.string;

value_above180days : abap.string;

 

}

Errors-
Short Dump Error
403 Error

Questins
1. can i consume it into a cds view or a table
2. How to pass data based on custom logic written in class to custom cds entity

3. implementing filter for point 2

4. Custom entity can be mapped to CDS view

 

 

@Andre_Fischer @yevgen_trukhin @julieplummer20 @krishnanunni_sudhakaranpi @kaitorsten_schipper

entry.title: Announcing the Water & Wastewater Leadership Day 2024: Brussels, Belgium, December 5th
entry.content:
​Following the tradition established at the SAP Executive Value Network for Water and Wastewater companies which have taken place during the last years in Europe and Latin America, this is an international knowledge- sharing oriented forum for those interested on innovative strategies and technologies to enhance water resilience and drive digital transformation in their companies.

Water companies across the world are struggling to resist and recover from enormous issues derived of the climate change together with others more known as infrastructure challenges. Such resilience requires the implementation of strategies and technologies that ensure a continuous and reliable supply of clean water, even in the face of disruptions at an affordable cost.

​In close collaboration with companies like FARYS, technology partners like ESRI and System Integrators, we planned an agenda bringing practical and inspirational use cases to introduce insightful discussions about the use of AI technologies, implementation of smart metering platforms, leveraging spatial data and enable consistent intelligent reporting in order to improve companies’ capacity to adapt into changing conditions and support sustainable development:

  • Intro / Welcome
  • The importance of sector collaboration:  Flanders collaborative experience for innovation in the Water Sector:  strategy and benefits
  • Developing an AI strategy in a public company
  • Smart Water Platform: 
    • Use cases from business perspective
    • Leveraging data intelligence beyond smart metering: Water Balance, energy management and AI- driven cases

Lunch break

  • Suez test and analytics of digital water meters
  • ESRI on HANA DB:  advantages and possibilities comibining data from SAP S/4HANA
  • Leveraging spatial data to build strategic asset management 

​For those interested to join us at the SAP Belgium offices in Brussels and take part in our discussions, we have set up a registration website where you can register for the event and get the latest updates:  https://events.sap.com/evn-water-2024/en/home 

​We are looking forward to meeting you in Belgium for an exciting day!

entry.title: Re: Is there option to block personal devices from loggin...

This can be achieved by using Certificate-Based Logon.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/9af7170c8661101482cd8d8dce5e5949.html?locale=en-US

3351213 - How to Download and Setup Certificate-Based Logon
https://me.sap.com/notes/3351213

Alternatively Single Sign On can provide a similar effect.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/single-sign-on-sso-with-sap-business-bydesign/ba-p/13337088

entry.title: Re: Industry Sector field in Product Master in S4HANA Pub...
entry.content:
Hello @bavinash1 

The Industry Sector field is not available in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, unlike in the On-Premises or Private Cloud versions. To maintain industry-related details in the Public Edition, you can utilize the Material Group field as an alternative.

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Where can I start learning how to configure SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management learning journey.

Overview
This course guides you through the intricate landscape of SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management. This advanced solution is designed to streamline and simplify the management of clinical supplies in the pharmaceutical and life sciences industries. In this course, you will explore the architecture of SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management, delve into its product components, master data setup, and configuration possibilities. You will also gain insights into integration aspects with other solutions, empowering you with the knowledge needed to optimize the clinical supply chain efficiently.

Learning objectives

  • Explain the SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management architecture and product components.
  • Describe the integration aspects of SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management with other solutions.
  • Set up and manage master data, including the creation of the three main material types.
  • Analyze and apply different configuration possibilities within SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management to meet specific business needs.

Prerequisites

  • Introducing SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI
entry.content:
The header that includes the directory for the source file would be "CamelFileName" but it also includes the value of the file itself which can be accessed via header "CamelFileNameOnly".  That information would only be relevant for the source because no value would be present for the target since it would reference the same headers or any specific configuration that you provide.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Architecture Decisions for Supplier Payment Collaboration
entry.content: When evaluating the SAP Business Network (SBN) and especially SAP Business Network for Supply Chain Collaboration it is imperative to understand how the business model towards the suppliers are expressed. This is so because the SBN for SCC is based on the business model of Procurement of Service, if your client is working from any other business model like from a manufacturing business model then the SBN for SCC may not be an appropriate solution for them.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
I have avoided using that terrible notation for several years, but there are a couple of exceptions.  I use c_ for constants (read-only awareness), t_ for tables so I can distinguish lists from normal values or structured values, and there are s_ and p_ for select options and parameters in report screens.  Outside of that I provide meaningful names for what the content would represent within the flow of the program.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Start lean with RAP (Restful ABAP programming model) #1
entry.content: @dan_sul Hi Daniel, interesting - I suppose you are talking about the preview that you open from Service Binding in ADT - so kind of generated Fiori Elements frontend? I would suggest you try to test your oData service through GW Client transaction. If you look at this older blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-gateway-deep-insert-end-to-end-scenario/ba-p/13362494 you can follow it from the part where they do GET with expand and use the same payload for POST (again in GW Client). If you put breakpoints in your behavior implementation create and save methods, it should stop there. I personally used this lean RAP approach in multiple apps with odata V2 + freestyle UI5 frontend (two-way binding of views directly to oDataModel and submitChanges to trigger the deep insert oData call)  and it worked with no issues.

entry.title: Re: Start lean with RAP (Restful ABAP programming model) #1
entry.content: @ralphswurld Hi Ralph, you wouldn't call BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT from the RAP behavior implementation class at all. RAP framework will trigger the COMMIT WORK for you automatically (happens after the SAVE method).

entry.title: Re: Deleting Blog Post
entry.content:
Hello @Cornelia 

I don't think you can delete a published blog post by yourself. That is due to SAP community rules on data retention. You can either:

  • remove the content of the post or
  • ask a moderator to delete it.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: IBP inbound data integration for medium term demand planning process
entry.content:
Hello,

suppose business needs to run statistical demand planning process (medium-long term) in IBP once a month, for example. This means no real time nor frequent data integration is needed. Therefore, data needed in IBP (in particular, for 11V best practice) would be provided via file with Data integration jobs app. Forecast must be then integrated to S/4 HANA via outbound integration (4NO best practice).

Is the integration method via file (Data integration jobs) suitable to run this business process / are there businesses running in IBP without data integration from S/4 to IBP (3OF best practice) ? What could be the drawbacks of not activating inbound integration from S/4 to IBP ?

Many thanks for your support and best regards.

Giuseppe

                                <String>groupby</String>
                                <String>aggregate</String>
                                <String>concat</String>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="GroupableProperties">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>SalesOrder</PropertyPath>
                                <PropertyPath>SoldToParty</PropertyPath>
                                <PropertyPath>TransactionCurrency</PropertyPath>
                                <PropertyPath>CreationDate</PropertyPath>
                                <PropertyPath>SalesOrderType</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>

entry.title: Re: Advanced Log Space Management (ALSM) as of Db2 for LUW Version 11.5
entry.content:
Hi Michael,
we believe that ALSM can provides a benefit and we received feedback from other customers that they would prefer to have it switched ON as a default. However it is just the new default and you can still explicitly switch it off even in Db2 V12.1 .

db2set DB2_ADVANCED_LOG_SPACE_MGMT=OFF

 I do not think that you need to change your current LOGPRIMARY, LOGSECOND or LOGFILSIZ configuration. But SAP still recommends to use LOGSECOND=0 to avoid log file allocations. Since you need to provide the space in log_dir to hold your secondary log files anyways you could also increase LOGPRIMARY instead. As described in the blog ALSM tends to reduce the need for total log space but you should keep some additional free space in the log_dir to hold the extraction files if needed. 20% free space may be a good value to start with.

Please keep in mind that ALSM starts extraction only after around 80% of (LOGPRIMARY + LOGSECOND) is used. So it may not even kick in before secondary log files are used if LOGSECOND is high. 

It is not necessary to change MAX_LOG. This parameter keeps its meaning. However, a too low NUM_LOG_SPAN setting may prevent that ALSM extraction kicks in at all because of the 80% rule above. So you either should set NUM_LOG_SPAN to a value larger than (LOGPRIMARY + LOGSECOND) or you could set NUM_LOG_SPAN=0 and use a WLM THRESHOLD on UOWTOTALTIME instead ( CREATE THRESHOLD statement - IBM Documentation ). WLM thresholds can also be configured in DBACOCKPIT.
Regards

                Frank

entry.title: Re: custom splash screen for mdk branded client
entry.content:
Hi @RaulM,

my working example uses mipmap icons which is also recommended to my knowledge (check following page for details: https://developer.android.com/studio/write/create-app-icons)

Here the example splash_screen.xml from the drawable-nodpi folder:

<layer-list xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android">
    <item>
        <shape android:shape="rectangle">
            <solid android:color="#FFFFFF" />
        </shape>
    </item>
    <item
        android:drawable="@mipmap/icon"
        android:gravity="center" />
</layer-list>

You need to make sure that your icon.webp fiel is placed in the different mipmap-x directories (mipmap-hdpi, mipmap-mdpi, mipmap-xhdpi, ...) depending on the resolution. The Image Asset Studio tool in Android Studio (https://developer.android.com/studio/write/create-app-icons#access) is a good helper tool to scale your image to the different resolutions

entry.title: How to resolve "backend error occurred" in SAP GUI for HTML (WebGUI)
entry.content:
Hi Community,

This blog will help you on how to troubleshoot and resolve backend error issues in SAP GUI for HTML.

While navigating in SAP GUI for HTML or Fiori, you may face "backend error occurred" pop-up and can't proceed with your activity flow. On the contrary, the same scenario works fine with SAP GUI for Windows.

 

  • What is a backend error?

Most of backend errors are self explanatory, the "control not found" for example, means that the control does not exists in a screen when the problematic post request for the control is sent to backend.

"failed to fire action", "failed to set focus" and other errors may also be outlined in the backend error details.

Basically, these are programming errors that needs a correction from WebGUI side, most of them are already resolved in latest Kernel patches.

 

  • How to resolve the backend error?

WebGUI corrections are delivered with Kernel patches, SAP recommend you to install the latest Kernel SP available.

If you cannot update the complete kernel, it is enough to update the ITS related part (webgui corrections) of the kernel using the sapwebgui*.sar file as described in SAP Note 2412840.

 

  • Test yourself

If you have a sandbox system where you can play with, the Kernel can be updated for test purposes, just to see if the issue occurs there. You can find the step by step below:

Easiest way to test SAP GUI for HTML (WebGUI) with latest kernel patch

 

  • and if the issue persist in the Kernel latest patch?

If users are still facing the backend error in the latest Kernel patch, you should open a case with SAP Technical Support with the following information:

  • Exactly step by step to reproduce the problem
  • HTTP traces reproducing the issue (1816543 - How to create HttpWatch trace to troubleshoot ITS related problem)

This will help us to track the problem and provide a correction as soon as possible.

 

 

All the best,

Lucas Machado

entry.title: Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition | 2023 FPS02 Release
entry.content:
Welcome back to my blog post series about Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition!

On the 8th October 2024, SAP has released the second “Feature Pack Stack (FPS)”, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for the 2023 release.
For a comprehensive overview of selected innovation highlights across all lines of business, see the blog post 

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition | 2023 FPS02 Release – Part 1

In this blog post we will dive deeper into the innovations for Maintenance Management as part of the line of business Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Release 2023 FPS02. 

 

Table of Contents

Enhancements in area of Phase Model 

  • Skipping screening phase in reactive maintenance process
  • Enhanced configuration for assigning overall status profile
  • Evaluation of maintenance order before entering next subphase

Inspection Checklist Process

  • Inspection Checklist Process Enhancements
  • Integrated checklist-handling in Find Technical Objects and Find Maintenance Orders and Operations
  • New SAP Fiori app Manage Inspection Checklists

Closed Loop

  • Integrated, cross-app navigation from the Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders app and Find Maintenance Orders app through BAdI implementation
  • Cross-application navigation from maintenance-order processing to permit to work 
  • Recommendations from SAP Asset Performance Management in the SAP Fiori app “Create Maintenance Request”
  • Enhanced integration of SAP Asset Performance Management into task lists and maintenance items for SAP Fiori apps
  • Providing a catalog-based “Failure Effect” field in the SAP Fiori apps for maintenance requests
  • Enabling situation handling for maintenance task lists and maintenance plans 

Enhancements for Perform Maintenance Jobs app

  • Overview of Team assignments
  • Overview of PRT assignments
  • Overview of planned Services
  • Planned Work and Remaining Work
  • Business Catalog for Cross-App Navigation

Purchase Contract Details in Maintenance Orders and Task Lists

Improve accuracy of material costs in refurbishment process

AI-assisted Maintenance Order Recommendation

Mass change of custom fields using the “Manage Maintenance Plans” and “Manage Maintenance Items” apps

Mass Generation of Permit to Work Objects

More Information / Additional Links

 

Enhancements in area of Phase Model 

Configuring Requirements for Creating Phase-Enabled Orders - Skipping screening phase in reactive maintenance process

If you create maintenance requests and maintenance orders of a specific type, these maintenance requests and orders are processed according to phases and become part of a comprehensive end-to-end process. In this business process, a standard logic controls the prerequisites that must be fulfilled before you can create a
phase-enabled maintenance order in the Planning phase. The underlying SAP standard logic is as follows:

  • Maintenance orders of the Reactive Maintenance order type must always be based on an accepted
    maintenance request. The application logic of the order evaluates that the header notification has been accepted during the screening phase, i.e. system status APOK (Approval: OK) has been activated. In other words: You can only create a reactive maintenance order in the Planning phase if the corresponding maintenance request has the system status APOK (Approval: OK) and is in the Accepted (Request) subphase. As soon as the accepted maintenance request has been assigned to a maintenance order, it is renamed to "maintenance notification". This header notification requires a priority and a final due date.
    In the case that the header notification does not include this active system status, the reactive maintenance order will not be created.
  • Maintenance orders of the Proactive Maintenance order type do not necessarily need to be based on a
    maintenance request. They can also be created based on a maintenance plan, for example. If you base a proactive maintenance order on a maintenance request, the maintenance request does not need to have run through an approval process.

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Release 2023 FPS02, configuration and a Business Add-In (BAdI) is provided that allows you to overwrite the standard application logic:

The new Business Add-In (BAdI) BAdI: Processing of Phase-Enabled Maintenance Orders
(EAM_CREATE_PHASE_ENABLED_ORDER) and the Customizing activity Define Requirements for Creating
Phase-Enabled Orders (Customizing view V_EAM_NOTIFCNFG) allow you to determine whether maintenance
requests need to be approved before phase-enabled maintenance orders can be based on them.

With the Customizing view V_EAM_NOTIFCNFG and the BAdI method CREATE_PHASE_ENABLED_ORDER, you can determine different combinations of planning plants, notification types and order types for which you want to implement a business logic different from the SAP standard described above. On the level of the maintenance planning plant, you can determine for each combination of notification type and order type whether an accepted maintenance request is mandatory for creating the corresponding phase-enabled maintenance order. For example, you can determine that in a specific planning plant, reactive maintenance orders can be created based on maintenance requests that have not been accepted. Or you can determine that proactive maintenance orders only can be created based on accepted maintenance requests.

In the example below,

  • a reactive maintenance order of type YA01 and maintenance planning plant 1010 can be raised against a notification of type M1. 
  • the notification Type M1 has no Approval Required flag active, so that any notification of that type will not enter the screening phase at all. Hence, those notification will never have system status APOK (Approval: OK) active.
  • without this configuration, the reactive maintenance order could not have been raised against a notification of type M1.

You can access the corresponding activity in Customizing: Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Maintenance and Service Processing -> Fiori Apps for Maintenance Processing -> General Settings -> Define Requirements for Creating Phase-Enabled Orders

Figure 1: Customizing - Change View “View for controlling the creation of phase-enabled orders”: Overview

In addition, see Customizing: Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Maintenance and Service Processing -> Maintenance and Service Notifications -> Overview of Notification Type

Figure 2: Customizing - Change View “Partner Functions, Approval”: Details

In the case that the configuration does not fulfil your business requirements, you can implement the BAdI EAM_CREATE_PHASE_ENABLED_ORDER. If you have entered the corresponding data in the Customizing view, the BAdI is called in the following cases:

  • You create a phase-enabled maintenance order in the Create Maintenance Order app.
  • You create a phase-enabled maintenance order in the Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders app.
  • You create a phase-enabled maintenance order via the BAPI BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN. 

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

Enhanced configuration for assigning overall status profile

If you process your maintenance orders according to phases, your business transactions lead to specific system statuses that are set on maintenance order or order operation level. These system statuses determine
the subphases and phases to which the corresponding maintenance order or order operation is passed. The subphases with their conditions and sequence throughout the maintenance process are bundled in an
overall status profile that is assigned to an order type. We deliver a standard configuration for the Reactive
Maintenance and Proactive Maintenance process.

Up to now, you could only specify the overall status profile depending on the order type.

Figure 3: Prior 2023 FPS02 release: Customizing view - Assign Overall Status Profile to Order Type

However, you might need to modify this standard configuration slightly depending on your various
maintenance planning plants. With the 2023 FPS02 release, you can also specify the valid overall status profiles depending on a combination of order type and maintenance planning plant.

Figure 4: Starting with 2023 FPS02 release: Customizing view - Assign Overall Status Profile to Order Type

You can access the corresponding activity in Customizing: Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Maintenance and Service Processing -> Fiori Apps for Maintenance Processing -> General Settings -> Configure Overall Status Profiles

Both screenshots shown above have the same consequence, all maintenance orders of type YA01 regardless of their maintenance planning plant will be assigned to the same overall status profile PMSP1.

Note:

  • Following restriction note 3365558, the configuration of the phase model is delivered by SAP and customers are not allowed to modify this configuration.
  • Future enhancements might remove some restrictions, so that customers might have several overall status profiles assigned to different combinations of maintenance planning plant and order types.

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

Evaluation of maintenance order before entering next subphase

A phase-model enabled maintenance order is assigned to an overall status profile which defines the conditions (using system statuses) determining each subphase of the order header and operation.

The new Business Add-In (BAdI) BAdI: Evaluation of Maintenance Order Data Including Buffer
(EAMS_EVAL_ORDER_DETAILS) allows you to evaluate data of phase-enabled maintenance orders each time
the maintenance orders are created or passed to a new subphase. This enables customers to introduce their custom logic to prevent the transition of the maintenance order into the next subphase.

As an example, a maintenance order has been released and the execution of this business transaction would determine a new subphase so that this order would be placed in the preparation phase.
Due to custom logic implemented in that BADI and the actual data of that maintenance order, the execution of this business transaction can be blocked so that this order remains in the current phase/subphase.

The BAdI reads maintenance order data from the buffer before the order data is saved on the database. You can evaluate this maintenance order data and implement warning or error messages that prevent the system from saving the order changes from the buffer to the data base. Note that you cannot use this BAdI to directly change any order data.
With this BAdI, you can evaluate and compare the following maintenance order data:

  • Order header data
  • Order operation data
  • Order operation component data
  • Data of the order object list
  • Phase control codes set on order header and on order operation level

This BAdI is called if you process phase-enabled maintenance orders

  • on the SAP Web User Interface for Plant Maintenance (PM)
  • in SAP Fiori apps
  • via the BAPI BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN
  • via the RAP-based API Maintenance Order (Version 2)

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

Inspection Checklist Process

For using the inspection checklist process

  • the business function LOG_EAM_CHECKLIST must be activated,
  • the checklist functionality must be enabled.

Check out also the Expert Talk: Inspection Checklist in Maintenance Management – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

We launched the inspection checklist solution first with the 2021 release and enhanced it with 2023 FPS01 release. What's new with FPS02?

Inspection Checklist Process Enhancements

In this chapter the description of each enhancement starts with a figure about the Phase Model for the Maintenance Process to provide you the context for phase-enabled orders. 

Maintenance Order: New Form Template for the Output Management

Figure 5: Phase Model for the Maintenance Process

With this feature, you can enhance a maintenance order by a new output type (CHECKLIST_REPORT for application object type MAINTENANCE_ORDER) and a new form and email template (EAM_MJP_CHECKLIST_REPORT / EAM_MJP_CL_DFLT_TMPL) . This enhancement is supported both by the classic SAP GUI as well as by WebDynpro.

Figure 6: 'Change Maintenance Order' app - new Output Type and Form Template

The new form template is an enhanced version of the old form with the following improvements:

  1. Barcode information is available for the checklist and is printed.
  2. Information on order and selected operations are printed.
  3. Checklist type of the operation is printed.
  4. Control indicator gets printed:
    + Required characteristic
    + Optional characteristic
    + After accepted characteristic
    + After rejected characteristic
    + Calculated characteristic (informational)
  5. Inspection characteristic and control indicator is printed together to avoid single lines
    at the end of a page

Figure 7: Maintenance Order: New Form Template for the Output Management

Note:

  • Deactivated and canceled checklists are not printed.
  • Inspection characteristics with the control indicators Do Not Print and No Charc Recording are not printed.
  • The fom template is translated.

 

Work Pack: New Form Template for Output Management

Figure 8: Phase Model for the Maintenance Process

With this feature, you can enhance the standard inspection checklist process for Work Pack Creation or Print in the Find Maintenance Order (F2175) and Find Maintenance Order and Operation (F2173) apps. With the new form and email template (PM_ORD_CHECKLIST_REPORT / EAM_MAINT_ORD_CL_DFLT_TMPL), the new output type (CHECKLIST_REPORT for application object type MAINTENANCE_JOB_PACKAGE), and the new grouping criterion (Operation Checklist Type), you can bundle job packs based on the checklist type functionality of order operations.

Figure 9: 'Find Maintenance Order' app - New grouping attribute 'Operation Checklist Type'

Figure 10: Work Pack - new Output Typ, Form Template, and Grouping Attribute

The new form template is an enhanced version of the old form with the following improvements (find the numbering also on the screenshot below):

  1. Barcode information is available for the checklist and is printed.
  2. Work pack/job pack information is printed.
  3. Checklist type of the operation is printed.
  4. Control indicator gets printed:
    + Required characteristic
    + Optional characteristic
    + After accepted characteristic
    + After rejected characteristic
    + Calculated characteristic (informational)
  5. Inspection characteristic and control indicator is printed together to avoid single lines
    at the end of a page

Figure 11: Work Pack / Job Pack - New Form Template for the Output Management

Note:

  • Deactivated and canceled checklists are not printed.
  • Inspection characteristics with the control indicators Do Not Print and No Charc Recording are not printed.
  • The fom template is translated.

 

Perform Maintenance Job app (F5104A)

Figure 12: Phase Model for the Maintenance Process

With the Perform Maintenance Job app you can now create follow-on notifications for inspection checklists by selecting the new button (see red dotted line in the screenshot below) in the checklists table of a job. The popover that is displayed when you select the status bar in the checklists table now shows additional details, including the usage decision valuation of the relevant inspection lot (see green dotted line). You can also display the usage decision valuation in the results table (see yellow dotted line) to inform the technician, if the inspection lot is still open for results recording.

Figure 13: 'Perform Maintenance Job' app enhancements

The Create Follow-On Notification uses the Notification Type from the Configure Checklists for Maintenance Order Types and Planning Plants activity and the technical object from the inspection lot.  A reference to the notification in the document flow is created.

Figure 14: 'Configure Checklists for Maintenance Order Types and Planning Plants' activity

Note: The Create Follow-On Notification is disabled, if the inspection lot is deactivated or has a recorded usage decision.

 

Final Time Confirmation: New system check of operations with checklists

New customizing flag Check Results Recording Status at Final Confirmation is available in customizing activity Configure Checklists for Maintenance Order Types and Planning Plants.

Figure 15: Customizing activity 'Configure Checklists for Maintenance Order Types and Planning Plants'

When the flag is set then the system checks if results have been recorded for all required or processed inspection characteristics. This check gets bypassed for deactivated, canceled or usage decision valuated inspection lots.

Final time confirmation check failed in IW41 transaction:

Figure 16: Final time confirmation check failed in IW41 transaction

Final time confirmation check failed in Perform Maintenance Jobs app:

Figure 17: Final time confirmation check failed in 'Perform Maintenance Jobs' app

 

Inspection Lot Usage Decision: New Follow-Up Actions

Figure 18: Phase Model for the Maintenance Process

With the new Follow-Up Actions you can enable the system to automatically change the system status of a technical object during checklist completion. For this purpose, three new function modules are available that you can specify as follow-up actions for usage decisions in Customizing. The function modules either set or reset the new technical object system status FLCO (Faulty Condition).

The following new function modules are available in function group EAM_CL_FOL:

  • EAM_CL_SET_TOBJ_STATUS_FLCO (Checklists Follow-Up Action: Set FLCO status on technical object)
    This follow-up action can be used to set the new technical object status FLCO independently of the inspection lot usage decision valuation:
    - The FLCO status becomes active, if it is not active.
    - You can use this follow-up action for example to accept an inspection lot based on visual characteristics, but the technical object has a technical faulty condition.
  • EAM_CL_RES_TOBJ_STATUS_FLCO (Checklists Follow-Up Action: Reset FLCO status on technical object)
    This follow-up action can be used to reset the new technical object status FLCO independently of the inspection lot usage decision valuation:
    - The FLCO status becomes inactive, if it is not inactive.
    - You can use this follow-up action for example to reject an inspection lot based on visual characteristics, but the technical condition is still valid.
  • EAM_CL_CHG_TOBJ_STATUS_FLCO (Checklists Follow-Up Action: Set or reset FLCO on tech obj based on UD) 
    This follow-up action can be used to dynamically set or remove the system status FLCO for the technical object of an inspection lot based on a predefined logic that considers the inspection lot valuation: A Rejected usage decision sets the status FLCO on the technical object. An Accepted usage decision removes the status FLCO from the technical object.

Note that the system status FLCO is informational in nature and does not trigger any business transactions on its own. However, you can control the execution of specific business transactions as a consequence of this status by defining custom user statuses that react to the setting and resetting of this status.

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

Integrated checklist-handling in Find Technical Objects and Find Maintenance Orders and Operations

Find Technical Objects app (F2072)

The Find Technical Objects app offers now the new filter group Checklists that enables you to find technical objects for which inspection checklists have been created and to view the open checklists for a technical object. 
The new Has Checklist column in the results table highlights the inspection relevance.
The new Open Checklists column in the results table shows the number of inspection lots for which no usage decision has been made. By clicking on the number, you can view the corresponding inspection lots and open each of them in the Record Inspection Results app (F1685A) or you can navigate to the Manage Inspection Checklists app (F6607) by clicking on the feeter line of the Open Checklists popover. If no open checklists exist, the system navigates to the Manage Inspection Checklists app (F6607) and displays a prefiltered list of all closed inspection lots with reference to that technical object. This cross-app navigation requires authorization for the apps F1685A and F6607.

Figure 19: 'Find Technical Objects' app (F2072) – List Page

Open Checklists will be displayed also in the object page header of the Find Technical Object app. The next two figures show you two different cases of the cross-app navigation from the Technical Object object page to the Manage Inspection Checklists app by clicking on the number of Open Checklists number:

Case 1: Number of Open Checklists greater than zero will show inspection lots without usage decision in Manage Inspection Checklists app (F6607).

Figure 20: 'Find Technical Objects' app (F2072) – Object Page - Cross-app navigation to 'Manage Inspection Checklists' app

Case 2: Number of Open Checklists equal to zero will show all inspection lots in Manage Inspection Checklists app (F6607).

Figure 21: 'Find Technical Objects' app (F2072) – Object Page - Cross-app navigation to 'Manage Inspection Checklists' app

New SAP Fiori app Manage Inspection Checklists

The new SAP Fiori app Manage Inspection Checklists (F6607) allows you to view and manage inspection checklists that have been created with reference to maintenance order operations, technical objects, and inspection lots.

You can display and process the results list on the level of individual inspection checklists (inspection lots) and inspection characteristics.
The results list provides detailed information about the relevant inspection lots and inspection characteristics, including the results recording status and usage decision valuation. In addition, it enables you to perform the following actions:

  • Record usage decisions
  • Copy or replace inspection lots
  • Activate or deactivate inspection lots
  • Navigate from an inspection lot to the Display Inspection Lot app (F2180)
  • Navigate from an inspection characteristic to the Record Inspection Results app (F1685A)
  • Navigate to other related apps by means of smart links in the results list

The Inspection Lot Details popover displays inspection lot operation (see red dashed box) above the inspection characteristics with their valuation status:

Closed Loop

Integrated, cross-app navigation from the Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders app and Find Maintenance Orders app through BAdI implementation

The new Business Add-In (BAdI) Cross-App Navigation Control for Fiori Applications (EAM_CROSS_APP_NAV_CONTROL) allows you to display custom cross-app navigation links in the following SAP Fiori apps:

  • Find Maintenance Orders (F2175)
  • Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders (F4604)

The custom cross-app navigation allows you to open selected items in other Fiori apps, including apps that are
not part of SAP S/4HANA.

When you create an implementation, the additional quick action Open In becomes available in the table toolbar of the app. Business users can use this quick action to display a dropdown menu with links to the target applications that you have defined in the implementation.
In your implementation, you can define relevant target applications, link texts, and system behavior of the
cross-app navigation according to your requirements. If a target application is not included in the business
catalog assignment of a user, the respective link will not be visible.

The BAdI is available for Key User Extensibility in the Custom Logic app for for business context:

  • Asset Management: Maintenance Order
  • Maintenance Notification

The BAdI is filter enabled and is initial working for Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders (F4604).

The screenshot below shows the creation of a new implementation of BAdI EAM_CROSS_APP_NAV_CONTROL with the 

  1. Business Context
  2. Dedicated BAdI
  3. BAdI Filter

Figure 30: 'Custom Logic' app - BAdI EAM_CROSS_APP_NAV_CONTROL for cross-app navigations

As an example, the next figure shows the usage of the implemented BAdI. See the following explanations of the numbers in the figure:

  1. Open In will be available, if an active BAdI-implementation is available and at least one authorization* for a cross-app navigation. * Authorization means an assigned PFCG role with a Fiori business catalog, that contains the target mapping (intent-based navigation)
  2. Translated individualized cross-app navigation labels possible by using Custom Code list from F3248
  3. Cross-app navigation label will be derived from the target mapping title
  4. Individualized cross-app navigation label is possible by using the optional actionName parameter
  5. If end users do not have the authorization* for a cross-app navigation, then this is not available. Device types of traget mappings are also considered for cross-app navigation list entries (supported by standard sapui5 function isNavigationSupported)

Figure 31: Cross-app navigation from the Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders app through BAdI implementation

Cross-application navigation from maintenance-order processing to permit to work 

Now, you can easily create a new work permit or view assigned work permits while processing a maintenance order on the SAP Web User Interface for Plant Maintenance.
When you open a maintenance order in the Change Maintenance Order app or the Display Maintenance Order app, a new navigation option is available in the Additional Functions menu. This option allows you to open the Manage Work Permits app in a new window and perform the following tasks:

  • Create a new work permit with reference to the maintenance order. The planning plant and the order
    number are copied from the maintenance order to the work permit creation dialog. If no work permit is
    assigned to the maintenance order, the system opens the work permit creation dialog by default.
  • View and manage the work permits that are associated with the maintenance order.
  • Remove the work permit assignments of the maintenance order.

The next figure show the cross-app navigation in case the order has no Work Permit assigned:

Figure 32: Cross-application navigation from maintenance-order processing to permit to work

  1. Customizing Change View Maintain Permit to Work Profile for Planning Plant (SM30: VPTWPROFILE)
  2. If the Permit to Work Customizing is enabled for the planning plant, then the cross-app navigation is available
  3. If the order has no Work Permit assigned, then the „Create Work Permit Request“ F4691 app is used for the cross-app navigation
  4. Create Work Permit Request app F4691 opens in a new window with the parameter passing of order number and planning plant

The next figure show the cross-app navigation in case the order has or had a Work Permit assigned:

Figure 33: Cross-application navigation from maintenance-order processing to permit to work

  1. Customizing Change View Maintain Permit to Work Profile for Planning Plant (SM30: VPTWPROFILE)
  2. If the Permit to Work Customizing is enabled for the planning plant, then the cross-app navigation is available
  3. If the order has or had a Work Permit assigned (PTW020/ PTW010), then the „Manage Work Permits“ F6579 app is used for the cross-app navigation and opens in a new window
  4. Prepopulate the order number and the planning plant to F6579 app
  5. Creating an additional Work Permit uses as well the parameter passing of order number and planning plant

Recommendations from SAP Asset Performance Management in the SAP Fiori app “Create Maintenance Request”

If you have set up the integration of SAP S/4HANA Asset Management with SAP Asset Performance
Management (SAP APM), you can let the system recommend relevant task lists based on the technical
object and the failure mode. With this feature, you can get recommendations for the task list in the Create
Maintenance Request (F1511A) app.

The next figure shows the integration scenario:

  1. Initiate Work Request
  2. Technical Object and/or Failure Data
  3. Propose Task Lists based on implemented Recommendations & addressed Failure Data
  4. Re-/Assignment of Task List based on  APM recommended Task List or S/4HANA value help
  5. Optional usage of the processing context emergency work will create an order in the execution phase with the exploded assigned task list

Figure 35: SAP APM Recommendation integration scenario: S/4HANA Create Maintenance Request (F1511A)

If you request maintenance for a specific technical object in the Create Maintenance Request app, you can
assign a task list to the maintenance request. With this feature, you can use the new Get Recommended Task Lists button to select the relevant task list from a list of recommendations generated by the SAP APM solution based on a combination of the technical object and the failure mode.

The next screenshot shows the Create Maintenance Request app with the following steps:

  1. The Task List field will be enabled, if the user has the authorization for the Emergency Work processing context (Authorization check against object I_EXCP_MN with field name ACTVT = 03 (processing context))
  2. The new action Get Recommended Task Lists will be available, if the business function is activated and the user has the Emergency Work processing context authorization. By the way: The button was renamed from Recommended Task List to Get Recommended Task Lists in Screen Maintenance Requests (F4072) app.
  3. Displaying additional information to support an easier task list choice.
  4. The recommended task lists are getting selected from the implemented APM Recommendations by using the:
    - Technical Object
    - Catalog Profile
    - Code Group
    - Failure Mode (Code)

Figure 36: S/4HANA Create Maintenance Request app

Providing a catalog-based “Failure Effect” field in the SAP Fiori apps for maintenance requests

Technicians can now record failure effects when processing maintenance requests in SAP Fiori. While the failure mode describes the way in which a technical object fails to perform a required function, the failure effect describes the evidence or the symptom that shows that a failure has occurred.
The failure effect is part of the failure data hierarchy in SAP Asset Performance Management and is now introduced in the apps for processing maintenance requests in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management. Several apps have now been enhanced with a new field, allowing you to capture this information in your maintenance
requests.
Technicians can report the failure effect while creating a maintenance request by selecting a suitable code from the catalog profile of the technical object or notification type. As a supervisor, you can change the failure effect after inspecting the situation on site. The specified failure effect is visible for maintenance technicians while performing the repair work.
This feature improves the quality of data that is recorded during failure detection and maintenance execution to facilitate reliable failure analytics and the development of maintenance strategies based on methodologies such as Reliability-Centered Maintenance (RCM) or Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA). It also further aligns the failure data model in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management with technical standards such as IEC 60300 and IEC 60812.

Customizing of catalog-based Failure Effect: Catalog Profile and Catalog

The figure below shows the customizing steps of catalog-based failure effect:

  3. Used catalog for the failure effect field during maintenance processing

Enabling situation handling for maintenance task lists and maintenance plans 

The extended framework of Situation Handling supports you in creating custom situations. Two kinds of situations are supported: object-based and message-based situations.
The following figure gives you an overview of the process flow for custom object-based situations, including the apps and roles that are involved:

Figure 48: Process Flow for Custom Object-Based Situations

For more information, see Situation Handling - Extended Framework.

Situation Handling now delivers the following situation templates for Maintenance Management:

  • Maintenance Plan (EAM_MAINTENANCE_PLAN) - This situation template allows you to create situation
    types that can trigger situations when a maintenance plan is created or changed. From the situation
    instance details, you can navigate to the related app Manage Maintenance Plans to view details of the plan.
  • Maintenance Item (EAM_MAINTENANCE_ITEM) - This situation template allows you to create situation
    types that can trigger situations when a maintenance item is created, assigned, or changed. From the
    situation instance details, you can navigate to the related app Manage Maintenance Items to view details of the item.
  • Maintenance Task List (EAM_MAINT_TASK_LIST) - This situation template allows you to create situation
    types that can trigger situations when a maintenance task list is created or changed. From the situation
    instance details, you can navigate to the related app Find Maintenance Task List to view details of the task
    list.

Based on the situation templates, you can create situation types in the Manage Situation Types - Extended app. In a situation type, you can define the conditions and rules based on which situation instances are created to notify you when a situation occurs. Choose a situation instance to view details of the situation. You are taken to the My Situations - Extended app. You can choose the related app to view details of the business object.

The figure below shows an example use case in the Manage Situation Types - Extended app with the following parameters:

  • Situation object:     EAM_MAINT_ITEM
  • Situation scenario:  EAM_MAINTENANCE_ITEM_PROCESSING
  • Situation template:  EAM_MAINTENANCE_ITEM

Figure 49: 'Manage Situation Types - Extended' app

The numbers in the figure above indicate the following:

Improve accuracy of material costs in refurbishment process

Business Context of Refurbishment Process related to Material Cost and Settlement

This feature enables you to make the system automatically settle an order of the order type refurbishment order (PM04) when it has been delivered to the warehouse. This is especially valuable if the materials have a moving average price.

In the settlement rule of the refurbishment order, only the inventory-managed material can be determined as the cost receiver. When the refurbishment is executed, actual costs incur as a result of consumption or confirmation postings. However, the moving average price of the material in the material master is recalculated not before the settlement of the refurbishment order. If the repairable spares have been refurbished and been delivered to the warehouse again but the refurbishment order has not yet been settled, the incorrect moving average price leads to incorrect planned and actual costs in maintenance orders that use this material as spare parts.

Improve accuracy of the moving average price of a material

With this feature, you can determine that the system automatically settles the refurbishment order each time when goods receipts have been posted for all units of the material. Only if all units of the material in the refurbishment order have been delivered to the warehouse, the Delivery Completed indicator is selected and the system status Delivered (DLV) is set on order header level. With the order settlement, the moving average price in the material master record is recalculated, the refurbished material in the warehouse is valued with the correct price, and as a result goods issues of the repairable spares are evaluated correctly. This leads to accurate planned and actual costs in the corresponding maintenance orders.

How to enable the automatic order settlement?

The following SAP Notes are required:

  • 3458343 - Business Event: Status of refurbishment order set to delivered
  • 3456964 - Automatic settlement of refurbishment orders

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

AI-assisted Maintenance Order Recommendation

​In the Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders app, you can now select a single notification and create a maintenance order using an existing order as a reference. Instead of selecting the reference order manually, you can let the system recommend suitable maintenance orders that have been created for similar issues in the past. The recommendations are generated using machine learning capabilities of SAP AI Services.

In the Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders app, the user interface for creating a new maintenance order has been enhanced. When you select a single maintenance notification and choose Create Order,

Figure 67: 'Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders' app - Create Order

the system displays a popup with a new Copy from Order field and a Get Recommendations button. ​

Figure 68: 'Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders' app - Create Order pop-up with active 'Get Recommendations' button

Once you have completed the required setup steps and created a trained machine learning model, this button is active and allows maintenance planners to retrieve recommendations for suitable reference orders that are generated by a machine learning algorithm.

​After you have pressed Get Recommendation the AI will propose the three best matching orders for your selected notification to copy from. You can expand this list up to six recommendations by pressing the More action.

Figure 69: 'Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders' app - Order Recommendations

Each recommended order is displayed as a card on the pop-up. The pop-up includes three pop-overs for Order, Technical Object, and Operations which should give the maintenance planner more details to the recommended orders. For instance, the next screenshot shows the pop-over for Operations:

Figure 70: 'Manage Maintenance Notifications and Orders' app - recommended order with pop-over for operations details

​If one of the recommended orders fits as a reference order for you, select the card and press Select.

Please note that for this innovation the commercialisation is pending (status: November 2024). Please contact your SAP account manager for more details.

Mass change of custom fields using the “Manage Maintenance Plans” and “Manage Maintenance Items” apps

With the Manage Maintenance Plans (F5325) app and the Manage Maintenance Items (F5356) app, you can now change custom fields of multiple maintenance plans and maintenance items respectively by choosing the Mass Change button. Previously, you were only able to perform mass changes on predefined fields. But now, you can also perform mass changes on custom fields that you have created. This flexibility allows you to tailor the system to your specific needs and streamline your maintenance processes.

First, we delivered this innovation with the 2408 release in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, see 2408 Blog Post. In the video below I show you the mass change of custom fields in the Manage Maintenance Plans app:

Mass Generation of Permit to Work Objects

With this feature, you can automatically generate work permits and any associated safety certificates for
maintenance orders using the program Mass Generation of Permit to Work Objects (transaction PTWMASSGEN).

The work permits are generated with reference to a work permit template and assigned to the respective
maintenance orders.
You can use various selection criteria to define the set of orders for which you would like to generate work
permits. This includes the possibility to generate additional work permits for orders with existing work permit
assignments. Furthermore, you can either specify a static work permit template or determine that the system
should select the appropriate work permit templates for each maintenance order automatically based on
the determination logic you have defined and the template assignments you have maintained for matching
combinations of order type, planning plant, technical object, and task list. The program also offers a test run
option to perform the relevant validations without creating any work permits.

Figure 71: Program 'Mass Generation of Permit to Work Objects' (transaction PTWMASSGEN)

Implementation Details

To use the automatic template determination, you need to perform the following steps:

  1. Define the template determination logic by implementing the new Business Add-In
    BADI_PTW_DTMN_TEMPLATES (BAdI: Template Determination for Generation of Work Permits). You can find this BAdI in Customizing for Plant Maintenance and Customer Service under Work Clearance
    Management Permit to Work Enhancements. SAP provides an example implementation with a
    predefined determination logic. The BAdI allows you to create different implementations depending on
    the planning plant.
  2. Assign work permit templates to specific combinations of planning plant, technical object, task list, and
    order type using the mapping table in transaction PTWDTMNTMPL.

Customizing
In order to use the mass generation of permit to work objects, the following settings are required in the relevant Customizing activities under Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Work Clearance Management -> Permit to Work :

  • You have defined internal number ranges for work permits and safety certificates.
  • You have defined a reference work permit type for the relevant work permit templates and a reference
    safety certificate type for the relevant safety certificate templates.
